Types: Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices
Title: CDET-001: IAM backdoor user created outside approved provisioning pipeline
Description: User arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/attacker created a new IAM user named backdoor-svc-acct at 2024-01-15T02:14:33Z from IP 192.0.2.45. This action was not initiated by the approved DeploymentPipelineRole and occurred outside of scheduled maintenance windows, indicating potential persistence via IAM backdoor account creation.

Recommendation.Text: Immediately disable and investigate the newly created IAM user backdoor-svc-acct. Review all access keys and console passwords attached to this account. Audit recent activity by the creating principal arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/attacker and revoke credentials if compromise is confirmed.

StatusReasons.Description: IAM user created outside approved pipeline by non-pipeline principal from anomalous IP address.

Note.Text: Correlated with validation_test_dataset.ndjson positive_CDET-001 event. Attacker IP 192.0.2.45 (TEST-NET) also associated with CDET-003, CDET-006, CDET-010, and CDET-013 events — same session.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices
Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards/CIS-AWS-Foundations-Benchmark
Title: CDET-003: CloudTrail logging stopped — audit trail impaired
Description: User arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/attacker called StopLogging on trail arn:aws:cloudtrail:us-east-1:123456789012:trail/org-management-trail at 2024-01-15T02:22:17Z from IP 192.0.2.45. Disabling CloudTrail logging is a key defense evasion step that eliminates the audit trail for subsequent attacker activity. This finding occurred 7 minutes after CDET-001 (IAM backdoor user creation) from the same source IP, indicating an active attack campaign.

Recommendation.Text: Immediately re-enable CloudTrail logging by calling StartLogging on the affected trail. Investigate all API activity that occurred during the logging gap. Restrict StopLogging permissions to only the break-glass emergency role and enforce CloudWatch alarms on trail status changes.

StatusReasons.Description: CIS AWS Foundations Benchmark 2.1 requires CloudTrail to be enabled in all regions. StopLogging directly violates this control.

Note.Text: Part of multi-stage attack sequence. CDET-001 fired 7 min prior (same source IP 192.0.2.45). Recommend correlating with CDET-006 (S3 policy modification) and CDET-010 (KMS key disabled) that followed within 30 minutes.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/Industry and Regulatory Standards/CIS-AWS-Foundations-Benchmark
Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices
Title: CDET-009: S3 cross-account replication rule added to sensitive bucket — potential data exfiltration
Description: User arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/attacker configured an S3 replication rule on bucket prod-sensitive-data-bucket that replicates objects to destination bucket arn:aws:s3:::exfil-bucket-external-acct in account 999888777666. This action was performed at 2024-01-15T02:55:00Z, 33 minutes after CloudTrail logging was disabled (CDET-003), suggesting deliberate audit evasion prior to data staging for exfiltration.

Recommendation.Text: Immediately delete the unauthorized replication rule from bucket prod-sensitive-data-bucket using DeleteBucketReplication. Audit all objects replicated to the destination account 999888777666 since 2024-01-15T02:55:00Z. Enforce SCP to block PutBucketReplication to external accounts. Rotate all credentials associated with arn:aws:iam::123456789012:user/attacker.

StatusReasons.Description: S3 replication rule added targeting external account 999888777666 not listed in approved destination account allowlist.

Note.Text: Third finding in correlated attack chain: CDET-001 (02:14Z) -> CDET-003 (02:22Z) -> CDET-006 (02:31Z) -> CDET-010 (02:45Z) -> CDET-009 (02:55Z) -> CDET-013 (02:50Z). All source IP 192.0.2.45. Recommend escalating to IR team for full campaign containment.

Types: Software and Configuration Checks/AWS Security Best Practices
